A, title V, § 541(c)136 Stat. 2580Dec. 23, 2022“The President shall prescribe regulations to ensure that residual prosecutorial duties and other judicial functions of convening authorities, including granting immunity, ordering depositions, and hiring experts, with respect to charges and specifications over which a special trial counsel exercises authority pursuant to , United States Code (article 24a of the Uniform Code of Military Justice) (as added by section 531 of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2022 (; )), are transferred to the military judge, the special trial counsel, or other authority as appropriate in such cases by no later than the effective date established in section 539C of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2022 (; note), in consideration of due process for all parties involved in such a case.”section 824a of title 10Public Law 117–81135 Stat. 1692Public Law 117–8110 U.S.C. 801

children.heading: .—Detail of Special Trial Counsel
children.label: a .—Detail of Special Trial Counsel
children.text: Each Secretary concerned shall promulgate regulations for the detail of commissioned officers to serve as special trial counsel.

children.label: A
children.text: is a judge advocate who is qualified under (article 6(a)(1)); andsection 806(a)(1) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the Judge Advocate General of the armed force of which the officer is a member; or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of the Marine Corps, the Staff Judge Advocate to the Commandant of the Marine Corps; and
in the case of a lead special trial counsel appointed pursuant to , is in a grade no lower than O–7.section 1044f(a)(2) of this title

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Special trial counsel shall carry out the duties described in this chapter and any other duties prescribed by the Secretary concerned, by regulation.

children.heading: Determination of covered offense; related charges.—
children.label: 2 Determination of covered offense; related charges.—

children.heading: .—Authority
children.label: A .—Authority
children.text: A special trial counsel shall have exclusive authority to determine if a reported offense is a covered offense and shall exercise authority over any such offense in accordance with this chapter. Any determination to prefer or refer charges shall not act to disqualify the special trial counsel as an accuser.

children.heading: .—Known and related offenses
children.label: B .—Known and related offenses
children.text: If a special trial counsel determines that a reported offense is a covered offense, the special trial counsel may also exercise authority over any offense that the special trial counsel determines to be related to the covered offense and any other offense alleged to have been committed by a person alleged to have committed the covered offense.

children.label: A
children.text: on behalf of the Government, withdraw or dismiss the charges and specifications or make a motion to withdraw or dismiss the charges and specifications;

children.label: B
children.text: refer the charges and specifications for trial by a special or general court-martial;

children.label: C
children.text: enter into a plea agreement; and

children.label: D
children.text: determine if an authorized rehearing is impracticable.

children.heading: .—Binding determination
children.label: 4 .—Binding determination
children.text: The determination of a special trial counsel to refer charges and specifications to a court-martial for trial shall be binding on any applicable convening authority for the referral of such charges and specifications.

children.heading: .—Deferral to commander or convening authority
children.label: 5 .—Deferral to commander or convening authority
children.text: If a special trial counsel exercises authority over an offense and elects not to prefer charges and specifications for such offense or, with respect to charges and specifications for such offense preferred by a person other than a special trial counsel, elects not to refer such charges and specifications, a commander or convening authority may exercise any of the authorities of such commander or convening authority under this chapter with respect to such offense, except that such commander or convening authority may not refer charges and specifications for a covered offense for trial by special or general court-martial.

children.heading: Special Trial Counsel Authority Over Certain Other Offenses.—
children.label: d Special Trial Counsel Authority Over Certain Other Offenses.—
A
children.text: An offense under section 917a (article 117a), 918 (article 118), section 919 (article 119), section 919a (article 119a), section 920 (article 120), section 920a (article 120a), section 920b (article 120b), section 920c (article 120c), section 928b (article 128b), or the standalone offense of child pornography punishable under section 934 (article 134) of this title that occurred on or before .December 27, 2023

children.label: B
children.text: An offense under section 925 (article 125), section 930 (article 130), or section 932 (article 132) of this title that occurred on or after , and before .January 1, 2019December 28, 2023

children.label: C
children.text: An offense under section 920a (article 120a) of this title, an offense under section 925 (article 125) of this title alleging an act of nonconsensual sodomy, or the standalone offense of kidnapping punishable under section 934 (article 134) of this title that occurred before .January 1, 2019

children.label: D
children.text: A conspiracy to commit an offense specified in subparagraph (A), (B), or (C) as punishable under (article 81).section 881 of this title

children.label: E
children.text: A solicitation to commit an offense specified in subparagraph (A), (B), or (C) as punishable under (article 82).section 882 of this title

children.label: F
children.text: An attempt to commit an offense specified in subparagraph (A), (B), (C), (D), or (E) as punishable under (article 80).section 880 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the offense occurs after , and on or before ; andJanuary 26, 2022January 1, 2025

children.label: ii
children.text: a formal complaint is substantiated in accordance with regulations prescribed by the Secretary concerned.

children.label: B
children.text: A conspiracy to commit an offense specified in subparagraph (A) as punishable under (article 81).section 881 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: A solicitation to commit an offense specified in subparagraph (A) as punishable under (article 82).section 882 of this title

children.label: D
children.text: An attempt to commit an offense specified in subparagraph (A), (B), or (C) as punishable under (article 80).section 880 of this title

children.heading: .—Treatment as covered offense
children.label: A .—Treatment as covered offense
children.text: If a special trial counsel exercises authority over an offense pursuant to paragraph (1) or (2), the offense over which the special trial counsel exercises authority shall be considered a covered offense for purposes of this chapter.

children.heading: .—Known or related offenses
children.label: B .—Known or related offenses
children.text: If a special trial counsel exercises authority over an offense pursuant to subsection (c)(2)(A) or paragraph (1) or (2) of this subsection, the special trial counsel may exercise the authority of the special trial counsel under subparagraph (B) of subsection (c)(2) with respect to other offenses described in that subparagraph without regard to the date on which the other offenses occur.
